Download and process Soil Bioclimatic and Temperature layers

soilclimate.Rd

This function downloads, processes, and extracts soil bioclimatic variables and monthly soil temperatures from the Global Soil Temperature dataset (Lembrechts et al., 2022).

Usage

soilclimate(x, vars, depth = "0-5", ...)

Arguments

x

The output from `par_set()` defining the area or locations for extraction, the reference system, and the buffer. Leave this empty and use `par_set()` to define parameters for download.

vars

Character vector of one or more variables to download and process.

depth

Character string defining the soil depth range. Options are "0-5" (default) or "5-15".

...

Additional arguments (currently unused).

Value

If `par_set()` contained a raster/polygon/points with buffer: a `SpatRaster` stack of processed variables. If `par_set()` contained spatial points or data.frame of points without buffer: a `data.frame` of x, y, and extracted values.

Details

Available variables (working synonyms in parentheses):

Bioclimatic Variables

  • SBIO1 - "SBIO1" [Â°C] ("annual mean temperature", "annual mean", "amt")

  • SBIO2 - "SBIO2" [Â°C] ("mean diurnal range", "mean diurnal", "mdr")

  • SBIO3 - "SBIO3" [Ratio] ("isothermality")

  • SBIO4 - "SBIO4" [SD] ("temperature seasonality", "seasonality")

  • SBIO5 - "SBIO5" [Â°C] ("max temperature warmest month", "max temp", "warmest month")

  • SBIO6 - "SBIO6" [Â°C] ("min temperature coldest month", "min temp", "coldest month")

  • SBIO7 - "SBIO7" [Â°C] ("temperature annual range", "annual range", "tar")

  • SBIO8 - "SBIO8" [Â°C] ("mean temperature wettest quarter", "wettest quarter")

  • SBIO9 - "SBIO9" [Â°C] ("mean temperature driest quarter", "driest quarter")

  • SBIO10 - "SBIO10" [Â°C] ("mean temperature warmest quarter", "warmest quarter")

  • SBIO11 - "SBIO11" [Â°C] ("mean temperature coldest quarter", "coldest quarter")

Monthly Mean Soil Temperatures

  • soilT01 - "soilT01" [Â°C] ("january mean", "january", "jan")

  • soilT02 - "soilT02" [Â°C] ("february mean", "february", "feb")

  • soilT03 - "soilT03" [Â°C] ("march mean", "march", "mar")

  • soilT04 - "soilT04" [Â°C] ("april mean", "april", "apr")

  • soilT05 - "soilT05" [Â°C] ("may mean", "may")

  • soilT06 - "soilT06" [Â°C] ("june mean", "june", "jun")

  • soilT07 - "soilT07" [Â°C] ("july mean", "july", "jul")

  • soilT08 - "soilT08" [Â°C] ("august mean", "august", "aug")

  • soilT09 - "soilT09" [Â°C] ("september mean", "september", "sep")

  • soilT10 - "soilT10" [Â°C] ("october mean", "october", "oct")

  • soilT11 - "soilT11" [Â°C] ("november mean", "november", "nov")

  • soilT12 - "soilT12" [Â°C] ("december mean", "december", "dec")

Citation:
Lembrechts, Jonas J., et al. (2022). "Global maps of soil temperature." Global Change Biology 28, no. 9: 3110-3144. https://doi.org/10.1111/gcb.16060

Note: Please cite original sources of primary datasets where appropriate.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
processed <- par_set(country= "Italy", crs=3035) %>% 
soilclimate(vars=c("SBIO1", "SBIO10"), depth="5-15")
   } # }
